mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 220 in the CPIAUCSL series signals central bank inaction, leading to real rate compression and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal or CPIAUCSL fails to exceed 220 in 3 consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in FEDFUNDS and rising CPIAUCSL indicate accommodative policy amid inflation, creating uncertainty that drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: SPX shows positive 60d returns in > 50% of instances where both FEDFUNDS < 0.2 and CPIAUCSL > 215, or the correlation between proxies exceeds 0.85.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production (INDPRO) signals weak supply-side capacity utilization, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days during expansionary regimes.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 55% of instances with INDPRO declines or INDPRO fails to correlate with SPX returns (Pearson < 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in UNRATE and PAYEMS indicate labor market overheating, prompting monetary tightening that drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX shows positive 5d returns in > 50% of instances where UNRATE < 9.0 and PAYEMS > 130,000, or the interaction term loses significance (p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts (HOUST) indicate strong demand for construction, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days during expansionary cycles.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 40% of instances with HOUST increases or HOUST fails to correlate with SPX returns (Pearson < 0.2).

mechanism_narrative: Weak real sales (RSAFS) combined with declining GDP (GDPC1) signals deteriorating demand, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days during contractionary regimes.
failure_condition: SPX shows positive 20d returns in > 50% of instances where RSAFS < 360,000 and GDPC1 < 13,200, or the interaction term becomes insignificant (p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: A 0.3pp monthly decline in UNRATE signals tightening labor market conditions, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days during expansionary phases.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 55% of instances with UNRATE declines > 0.3pp or UNRATE fails to correlate with SPX returns (Pearson < 0.15).
